title: loyalty program archetypes
purpose: starting-point templates for LLM to generate and customize loyalty programs per business. each archetype maps to a program type from loyalty_programs.json and is structured to match the loyalty_program_examples supabase table.
usage: when a recommendation is derived from onboarding_schema.json, load the matching archetype, then substitute business-specific values using llm_customization_hints as a guide.

supabase_schema.note: refined schema — additions and reasons documented below

columns.program_type: text — enum: 'points' | 'tiered' | 'cashback' | 'punch_card' | 'coalition' | 'points_tiers'. links archetype back to onboarding decision rules. NEW.
columns.industry: text — industry vertical this archetype is drawn from
columns.program_name: text — branded name of the program (e.g. 'Sip Rewards', 'The Inner Circle')
columns.currency_name: text — what the business calls its points/credit unit (e.g. 'Stars', 'Beans', 'Credits', '% back')
columns.earn_rules: jsonb — how customers earn: rate per dollar, qualifying actions, multipliers
columns.redemption_rules: jsonb — how and when earned currency can be spent. NEW. complements earn_rules — missing this makes the program incomplete.
columns.reward_tiers: jsonb — tier levels with names, thresholds, and perks. null for flat programs.
columns.tier_progression_rules: jsonb — how customers qualify for, maintain, and lose tiers. NEW. separates progression logic from tier perks for cleaner LLM reasoning.
columns.points_expiry_rules: jsonb — when points or credits expire and under what conditions. NEW. critical for SMB programs — expiry drives urgency and repeat visits.
columns.bonus_rules: jsonb — bonus earn events: double points days, birthday multipliers, referrals, first purchase
columns.referral_description: text — plain-language description of the referral mechanic if one exists
columns.brand_voice_summary: text — tone and personality the program should communicate (e.g. 'warm and community-focused', 'premium and exclusive')
columns.llm_customization_hints: jsonb — NEW. instructions for the LLM on which fields to vary based on business inputs, and what constraints to respect when customizing.
columns.terms_and_conditions: text — auto-generated legal terms for the program. covers enrollment, earning, redemption, expiry, modification rights, and liability. written in standard legal prose. NEW.

qualifying_actions.notes: double Sips on any purchase in birthday month

redemption_rules.notes: reward issued as store credit applied at checkout

points_expiry_rules.notes: inactivity = no purchase activity. expiry resets on any qualifying purchase.

bonus_rules.notes: applied automatically on first transaction post-signup
awarded when referred friend completes first purchase

archetypes.referral_description: share a personal referral link or code. earn 30 Sips when your friend signs up and makes their first purchase. your friend gets 20 Sips to start.
archetypes.brand_voice_summary: warm, community-focused, and approachable. the program should feel like a thank-you from a local business, not a corporate scheme. use first-name messaging and celebrate milestones.

llm_customization_hints.vary_by_industry: adjust qualifying_actions to match what the business sells — a bakery might award bonus points on weekend purchases; a gym might award points for class check-ins

vary_by_goal.acquire: increase welcome bonus and referral reward; lower minimum_to_redeem to show value faster
vary_by_goal.retain: add a streak bonus (e.g. 3 visits in a week = 2x points); tighten expiry to 90 days to drive urgency
vary_by_goal.revenue: add a spend-threshold bonus (e.g. earn 50 bonus Sips when total spend exceeds $200/month)

llm_customization_hints.vary_currency_name: rename 'Sips' to something on-brand for the business — a bookshop might use 'Pages', a pet store might use 'Paws'
keep_fixed: base redemption rate (100 pts = $5 is a 5% return — adjust only if margin allows)
partial redemption should stay true for ease of use

archetypes.terms_and_conditions:
PROGRAM NAME REWARDS — TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Last Updated: [DATE]

1. PROGRAM OVERVIEW
[Business Name] ("Company," "we," "us," or "our") operates the [Program Name] loyalty rewards program ("Program"). By enrolling in the Program, you ("Member," "you," or "your") agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions ("Terms"). These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and the Company.

2. ELIGIBILITY AND ENROLLMENT
2.1 The Program is open to individuals who are 18 years of age or older and residents of [jurisdiction].
2.2 Enrollment requires the provision of a valid name, email address, and any other information requested at the time of registration.
2.3 One account is permitted per individual. The Company reserves the right to merge or terminate duplicate accounts without notice.
2.4 Membership is personal and non-transferable. Corporate, business, or group enrollments are not permitted unless expressly authorized in writing by the Company.

3. EARNING [CURRENCY NAME]
3.1 Members earn [X] [Currency Name] for every $1.00 (USD) spent on qualifying purchases, rounded down to the nearest whole dollar.
3.2 Qualifying purchases exclude taxes, gratuities, gift cards, alcohol (where prohibited by applicable law), prior purchases, and any items expressly excluded at the Company's discretion.
3.3 Bonus [Currency Name] may be awarded for specific actions including, but not limited to, account creation, birthday month purchases, qualifying referrals, and promotional events. Bonus awards are subject to availability and may be modified or discontinued at any time without notice.
3.4 [Currency Name] have no cash value, are not transferable, and may not be sold, bartered, or exchanged outside of the Program.
3.5 The Company reserves the right to adjust earn rates, qualifying purchase categories, and bonus multipliers at any time. Changes will be communicated via the Company's website or app and will take effect on the date specified.

4. REDEMPTION
4.1 Members may redeem [Currency Name] for store credit or rewards once a minimum balance of [X] [Currency Name] has been accumulated.
4.2 A redemption value of [X] [Currency Name] equals $[X] in store credit, which may be applied toward qualifying purchases in-store or online.
4.3 [Currency Name] may not be redeemed for cash, applied retroactively to prior purchases, or used in conjunction with other discount programs unless expressly permitted.
4.4 Redemptions cannot be applied to purchases of alcohol (where restricted by applicable law), gift cards, or any other excluded categories as determined by the Company.
4.5 The Company reserves the right to set a maximum redemption amount per transaction.

5. EXPIRATION OF [CURRENCY NAME]
5.1 [Currency Name] expire after a period of 180 consecutive days of account inactivity. Inactivity is defined as the absence of any qualifying purchase transaction on the Member's account.
5.2 Any qualifying purchase resets the inactivity period in full.
5.3 The Company will endeavor to notify Members of impending expiration no fewer than 30 days prior to the expiration date via the email address on file. Failure to receive such notification does not invalidate the expiration of [Currency Name].
5.4 Expired [Currency Name] cannot be reinstated under any circumstances.
5.5 Upon termination of the Program, Members will be given no fewer than 30 days' notice to redeem outstanding [Currency Name] balances, after which all unredeemed [Currency Name] will be forfeited without compensation.

6. ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT
6.1 Members are responsible for maintaining the accuracy of their account information. The Company is not liable for [Currency Name] lost due to inaccurate contact information.
6.2 [Currency Name] balances are tracked via the Member's account and will be reflected within 24 hours of a qualifying transaction.
6.3 Members must notify the Company within 30 days of a transaction if [Currency Name] were not properly credited. The Company reserves the right to deny adjustments for claims submitted beyond this window.
6.4 In the event of a return or refund, [Currency Name] earned on the returned transaction will be deducted from the Member's balance. If insufficient [Currency Name] remain, the balance may become negative and must be offset before future redemptions are permitted.

7. FRAUD AND MISUSE
7.1 Any attempt to earn [Currency Name] through fraudulent, deceptive, or unauthorized means — including but not limited to fictitious transactions, unauthorized account access, or manipulation of the Program — will result in immediate account termination and forfeiture of all accumulated [Currency Name].
7.2 The Company reserves the right to audit any account and withhold or reverse [Currency Name] pending investigation.

8. PROGRAM MODIFICATIONS AND TERMINATION
8.1 The Company reserves the right to modify, suspend, or terminate the Program or any aspect thereof at any time, with or without notice.
8.2 Changes to earn rates, redemption values, expiry policies, or eligible categories will be posted on the Company's website and are effective upon posting unless otherwise stated.
8.3 Continued participation in the Program following the posting of changes constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
8.4 The Company may terminate a Member's account at its sole discretion for violation of these Terms, suspected fraud, or any conduct deemed harmful to the Program or other Members.

9. PRIVACY
9.1 By enrolling in the Program, Members consent to the collection and use of personal data as described in the Company's Privacy Policy, which is incorporated herein by reference.
9.2 Program participation data, including purchase history and [Currency Name] balances, may be used to personalize offers and communications.

10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
10.1 The Company's liability in connection with the Program shall not exceed the dollar value of [Currency Name] accrued in a Member's account at the time of the claim.
10.2 The Company is not responsible for technical failures, unauthorized account access, or [Currency Name] lost due to circumstances beyond its reasonable control.
10.3 The Program is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind, express or implied.

11. GOVERNING LAW
These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [State/Jurisdiction], without regard to its conflict of law provisions. Any disputes arising under these Terms shall be resolved in the courts of [State/Jurisdiction].

12. CONTACT
For questions regarding the Program or these Terms, please contact: [Business Name], [Address], [Email], [Phone].

archetypes.currency_name: annual spend (no named points currency — tier is spend-based)

earn_rules.base_rate: no points — tier status determined by cumulative annual spend

qualifying_actions.notes: all purchases count toward annual spend total
if applicable: spend counts at 1.5x toward tier qualification

earn_rules.notes: customers are always aware of their spend-to-next-tier gap via app or receipt

redemption_rules.mechanism: perks are automatic and tier-based, not redeemed manually
redemption_rules.notes: unlike points programs, customers do not redeem anything — benefits activate based on current tier. discounts apply at checkout automatically.

perks: birthday discount: 15% off one purchase in birthday month
early access to sale events (24 hours before public)
free standard shipping on orders over $75
free standard shipping on all orders (no minimum)
free expedited shipping on all orders
invitation to private events and product previews
personal stylist or product consultant access

tier_progression_rules.upgrade_timing: tier upgrades immediately when spend threshold is crossed
tier_progression_rules.downgrade_policy: tier is re-evaluated annually. if spend drops below threshold, customer drops one tier (not to bottom). given a 90-day grace period after renewal date before downgrade takes effect.
tier_progression_rules.downgrade_warning: notification sent 60 days before renewal date showing spend needed to maintain current tier
tier_progression_rules.starting_tier: Member — all enrollees start here regardless of spend history

points_expiry_rules.expiry_policy: not applicable — this is a spend-based tier program with no points currency

bonus_rules.reward: one-time 20% off welcome discount at new tier
bonus_rules.notes: applied automatically on first purchase after upgrade
rewards the referrer when the referred friend reaches Insider tier

archetypes.referral_description: refer a friend and earn a $25 store credit when they reach Insider status. your friend starts at Member with a 10% welcome discount on their first order.
archetypes.brand_voice_summary: elevated, aspirational, and insider-feeling. the program should make customers feel recognized and chosen, not just discounted. language should be warm but polished — 'you've earned your place' rather than 'congratulations on your points'.

vary_by_industry.hospitality & travel: replace spend thresholds with nights stayed or flights taken; replace product perks with room upgrades, late checkout, lounge access
vary_by_industry.health & beauty: replace product drops with early access to new treatments or product lines; add consultation perks at top tier
vary_by_industry.professional services: replace product perks with priority scheduling, dedicated account manager, reduced rates on premium services

vary_by_goal.acquire: lower the Member→Insider threshold to make first upgrade feel achievable quickly; add a visible progress tracker in onboarding
vary_by_goal.retain: tighten downgrade grace period; add a 'stay at your tier' spend nudge 60 days before renewal
vary_by_goal.revenue: widen the gap between Insider and Icon to stretch high-value customers further; add a spend incentive event mid-year

llm_customization_hints.vary_tier_names: rename tiers to match brand identity — a spa might use 'Glow / Radiance / Luminous'; a wine shop might use 'Explorer / Connoisseur / Sommelier'
llm_customization_hints.vary_threshold_amounts: calibrate thresholds to average order value — Icon tier should be reachable by roughly the top 10-15% of customers based on historical spend data
keep_fixed: downgrade grace period (90 days minimum — sudden downgrades cause churn)
automatic upgrade timing — never make customers claim their tier upgrade manually

archetypes.terms_and_conditions:
PROGRAM NAME MEMBERSHIP — TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Last Updated: [DATE]

1. PROGRAM OVERVIEW
[Business Name] ("Company") operates [Program Name] ("Program"), a tiered membership program that rewards customers based on annual qualifying spend. By enrolling, you ("Member") agree to these Terms and Conditions ("Terms").

2. ELIGIBILITY AND ENROLLMENT
2.1 The Program is open to individuals aged 18 or older residing in [jurisdiction].
2.2 Membership is personal and non-transferable. One account per individual is permitted.
2.3 The Company reserves the right to refuse or revoke enrollment at its sole discretion.

3. TIER STRUCTURE AND QUALIFICATION
3.1 The Program comprises the following membership tiers: [Tier 1 Name] (entry level), [Tier 2 Name], and [Tier 3 Name] (highest level). Tier names, thresholds, and associated benefits are subject to change at the Company's discretion.
3.2 Tier qualification is based on cumulative qualifying spend within a rolling 12-month period ("Qualification Period"), as follows:
    — [Tier 1 Name]: $0 qualifying spend (all enrolled Members)
    — [Tier 2 Name]: $[X] qualifying spend within the Qualification Period
    — [Tier 3 Name]: $[X] qualifying spend within the Qualification Period
3.3 Qualifying spend includes amounts paid for eligible products and services, net of returns, refunds, discounts applied at point of sale, taxes, and gratuities. Gift card purchases and any categories expressly excluded by the Company do not count toward tier qualification.
3.4 Where a Company-affiliated credit card is available, spend via that card may be credited at an accelerated rate toward tier qualification as specified in the applicable credit card program terms.

4. TIER UPGRADES
4.1 Tier upgrades take effect immediately upon the Member's cumulative qualifying spend crossing the applicable threshold within the current Qualification Period.
4.2 Benefits associated with a new tier are available from the date of upgrade.
4.3 A one-time welcome benefit may be issued upon first entry to [Tier 2 Name] or [Tier 3 Name] at the Company's discretion.

5. TIER MAINTENANCE AND DOWNGRADE
5.1 Tier status is reviewed annually at the end of each Member's Qualification Period ("Renewal Date").
5.2 To maintain a tier, a Member must meet or exceed the qualifying spend threshold for that tier within the 12-month Qualification Period ending on the Renewal Date.
5.3 Members who do not meet the spend threshold to maintain their current tier will be moved down one tier level (not to the entry tier, unless the Member fails to qualify for the tier immediately below their current tier).
5.4 A grace period of 90 days from the Renewal Date is provided before any downgrade takes effect. Benefits of the current tier remain available during the grace period.
5.5 The Company will use reasonable efforts to notify Members of tier downgrade risk no fewer than 60 days prior to the Renewal Date, using the contact information on file. Non-receipt of such notice does not affect the application of downgrade rules.
5.6 Downgraded Members may re-qualify for a higher tier at any time during the subsequent Qualification Period by meeting the applicable spend threshold.

6. TIER BENEFITS
6.1 Benefits associated with each tier are as described on the Company's website or in the Program materials at the time of enrollment or tier upgrade.
6.2 Benefits are non-transferable, have no cash value, and may not be combined with other promotional offers unless expressly stated.
6.3 The Company reserves the right to modify, substitute, or withdraw tier benefits at any time. Material changes to benefits will be communicated to affected Members via email or the Program portal with reasonable advance notice.
6.4 Birthday benefits are available during the Member's birthday calendar month only, are limited to one use per year, and require a qualifying purchase to activate.

7. RETURNS AND ADJUSTMENTS
7.1 Qualifying spend will be adjusted to reflect returned or refunded transactions. Returns may result in a Member's qualifying spend falling below a tier threshold, which may affect tier status at the next Renewal Date.
7.2 Spend adjustments take effect within 5 business days of the return or refund being processed.

8. FRAUD AND MISUSE
8.1 Any attempt to manipulate qualifying spend calculations, create fictitious transactions, or otherwise abuse the Program will result in immediate account termination and forfeiture of all tier benefits.
8.2 The Company reserves the right to investigate any account and withhold benefits pending the outcome of such investigation.

9. PROGRAM MODIFICATIONS AND TERMINATION
9.1 The Company reserves the right to modify, suspend, or terminate the Program at any time. In the event of Program termination, Members will be given no fewer than 30 days' advance notice.
9.2 Tier thresholds, tier names, and associated benefits may be changed at any time. Changes will be posted on the Company's website and are effective as of the date stated.
9.3 Continued participation following any modification constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

10. PRIVACY
10.1 Personal data collected through Program enrollment and participation is governed by the Company's Privacy Policy, incorporated herein by reference. Spend data may be used to personalize offers and determine tier status.

11. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
11.1 The Company's liability under the Program is limited to the correction of tier status errors or the provision of applicable tier benefits. The Company is not liable for indirect, consequential, or incidental damages arising from Program participation or termination.

12. GOVERNING LAW
These Terms are governed by the laws of [State/Jurisdiction]. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of [State/Jurisdiction].

13. CONTACT
[Business Name], [Address], [Email], [Phone]

archetypes.currency_name: store credit (displayed as $ value, not points)

qualifying_actions.notes: higher rate on store-brand or house-label items to drive margin-friendly behavior
small credit for engaging with the digital coupon system — drives app usage

earn_rules.credit_posting_delay: credit posts to account within 24 hours of purchase
earn_rules.notes: cashback is always displayed in dollar value, never converted to abstract points

redemption_rules.redemption_method: applied automatically at checkout or manually via app toggle
redemption_rules.maximum_redemption_per_transaction: no cap — full balance can be applied to a single transaction
redemption_rules.notes: customer can toggle auto-apply off in app settings if they prefer to save credit for larger purchases

points_expiry_rules.notes: longer expiry window than points programs — cashback customers are price-sensitive and longer windows reduce frustration. inactivity = no purchase.

bonus_rules.rate: 5% back on a rotating product category
bonus_rules.notes: e.g. 5% back on produce in January; 5% back on household goods in February. drives category exploration.
small flat bonus for hitting a monthly spend milestone — drives consolidation of grocery spend
instant $5 credit on first qualifying purchase — visible value immediately
both referrer and new member receive $5 credit after friend's first purchase

archetypes.referral_description: refer a friend and you both get $5 in store credit when they make their first purchase of $10 or more. there is no limit to how many friends you can refer.
archetypes.brand_voice_summary: straightforward, honest, and value-focused. never use jargon or gamification language. the program should communicate 'you save real money every time you shop here' — nothing more complicated. transparency about rates and balances builds trust with price-sensitive customers.

vary_by_industry.e-commerce: increase base rate to 3-5% to compete with credit card cashback; add a higher rate for repeat category purchases to encourage browsing
vary_by_industry.fuel & automotive: tie bonus rate to fuel fill-up frequency; add a fleet or multi-vehicle account option for business customers
vary_by_industry.financial services: frame as statement credit rather than store credit; integrate with card spend rather than a separate program enrollment

vary_by_goal.acquire: lead with the first-purchase bonus ($5 or 10% back on first order) in all acquisition messaging — this is the clearest hook for a cashback program
vary_by_goal.retain: add the monthly spend threshold bonus and surface a 'you're $X away from your $3 bonus' nudge in the app
vary_by_goal.revenue: increase the own-brand product rate (5%→8%) to shift basket composition toward higher-margin items

llm_customization_hints.vary_cashback_rate: calibrate base rate to gross margin — 2% is sustainable at 30%+ GM. for lower-margin businesses (grocery at 15-20% GM), 1% base with targeted 5% category bonuses is safer
keep_fixed: always display value in dollars, never in points — this is the core differentiator of a cashback program
auto-apply default should stay true — friction at redemption is the #1 reason cashback programs underperform
minimum redemption threshold should stay at $5 or below — higher minimums negate the 'immediate value' appeal

archetypes.terms_and_conditions:
PROGRAM NAME CREDIT — TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Last Updated: [DATE]

1. PROGRAM OVERVIEW
[Business Name] ("Company") operates [Program Name] ("Program"), a cashback rewards program that returns a percentage of qualifying purchase amounts to Members as store credit. By enrolling, you ("Member") agree to these Terms and Conditions ("Terms").

2. ELIGIBILITY AND ENROLLMENT
2.1 The Program is open to individuals aged 18 or older residing in [jurisdiction].
2.2 One account per individual. Accounts may not be shared, pooled, or transferred.
2.3 Enrollment by employees of the Company is subject to separate internal policies and may be restricted.

3. EARNING STORE CREDIT
3.1 Members earn store credit equal to [X]% of the pre-tax subtotal of each qualifying purchase, rounded to the nearest cent.
3.2 An enhanced rate of [X]% applies to purchases of [store-brand / house-label / specified] products, as identified at point of sale or on the Company's website.
3.3 Qualifying purchases exclude taxes, fees, gratuities, gift card purchases, lottery tickets, alcohol (where restricted by applicable law), tobacco products, and any other categories designated as excluded by the Company from time to time.
3.4 Store credit is posted to the Member's account within 24 hours of a qualifying transaction being completed and settled.
3.5 Store credit has no cash value, is non-transferable, and may not be sold, exchanged, or redeemed for cash under any circumstances.
3.6 Earn rates are subject to change at the Company's discretion. Changes will be communicated via the Company's website and take effect on the date posted.

4. BONUS STORE CREDIT
4.1 From time to time, the Company may offer bonus store credit for specified product categories, spend thresholds, promotional periods, or other qualifying events ("Bonus Events").
4.2 Bonus Event terms, including eligible categories, rates, and duration, will be published on the Company's website or communicated via the Member's registered email address.
4.3 A welcome credit of $[X] will be applied to a Member's account upon completion of their first qualifying purchase of $[X] or more. This welcome credit is available once per Member and is not available to Members who previously held an account with the Company.
4.4 Referral credits are issued in accordance with Section 5. All referral credits are subject to verification and may be reversed if the qualifying referral transaction is subsequently returned or voided.

5. REFERRAL PROGRAM
5.1 Members may refer new customers using a unique referral link or code provided through the Program portal.
5.2 A referral credit of $[X] will be awarded to the referring Member when the referred individual: (a) enrolls as a new Member; and (b) completes a qualifying purchase of $[X] or more within 30 days of enrollment.
5.3 The referred individual will receive a welcome credit of $[X] on the same conditions.
5.4 Self-referrals, and referrals to individuals who have previously held a Program account, are ineligible. The Company reserves the right to withhold or reverse referral credits where abuse or circumvention is suspected.
5.5 There is no limit on the number of eligible referrals a Member may make.

6. REDEMPTION OF STORE CREDIT
6.1 Store credit may be applied toward qualifying in-store or online purchases once a minimum balance of $[X] has accumulated in the Member's account.
6.2 Store credit is applied automatically at checkout by default. Members may disable auto-apply via the Program portal or app settings.
6.3 Partial use of the store credit balance is permitted. Any unused balance remains in the Member's account subject to the expiry provisions in Section 7.
6.4 Store credit may not be applied to gift card purchases, lottery tickets, alcohol (where restricted by applicable law), or other excluded items as determined by the Company.
6.5 Store credit applied to a transaction and subsequently returned will be reinstated to the Member's account, less any applicable handling fees, within 5 business days of the return being processed.

7. EXPIRATION OF STORE CREDIT
7.1 Store credit expires after 365 consecutive days of account inactivity. Inactivity is defined as the absence of any qualifying purchase transaction on the Member's account.
7.2 Any qualifying purchase resets the inactivity period in full from the date of that transaction.
7.3 The Company will use reasonable efforts to notify Members of impending credit expiration no fewer than 45 days prior to expiration, using the contact information on file. Failure to receive such notification does not invalidate the expiration of store credit.
7.4 Expired store credit is permanently forfeited and cannot be reinstated.
7.5 In the event of Program termination, Members will be given no fewer than 30 days to redeem outstanding store credit balances. Unredeemed balances at the close of that period are forfeited without compensation.

8. RETURNS AND CHARGEBACKS
8.1 Store credit earned on a returned purchase will be deducted from the Member's account at the time the return is processed. If the deduction results in a negative balance, future store credit accruals will offset the negative balance before being made available for redemption.
8.2 In the event of a chargeback or payment dispute, the Company reserves the right to deduct any store credit issued on the disputed transaction pending resolution.

9. ACCOUNT ACCURACY AND DISPUTES
9.1 Members are responsible for maintaining accurate account information. The Company is not liable for store credit lost due to outdated or incorrect contact information.
9.2 Members must report missing or incorrect store credit within 30 days of the relevant transaction. Claims submitted beyond this window may be denied at the Company's discretion.

10. FRAUD AND MISUSE
10.1 The Program is intended for genuine personal retail purchases. Commercial resale, fraudulent transactions, or systematic exploitation of promotional earn rates are prohibited and will result in account suspension, forfeiture of all store credit, and potential legal action.
10.2 The Company reserves the right to audit any account and withhold store credit pending investigation.

11. PROGRAM MODIFICATIONS AND TERMINATION
11.1 The Company may modify, suspend, or terminate the Program at any time. Material changes will be communicated to Members via the registered email address or the Company's website with reasonable advance notice.
11.2 Continued participation following any modification constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

12. PRIVACY
12.1 Data collected through Program enrollment and participation is subject to the Company's Privacy Policy, incorporated herein by reference. Purchase and credit data may be used for personalization and analytics purposes.

13. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
13.1 The Company's liability under the Program is limited to the value of store credit legitimately accrued and not yet redeemed at the time of a claim. The Company is not liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages of any kind.
13.2 The Program is provided without warranty. The Company does not guarantee uninterrupted access to Program features or earn rates.

14. GOVERNING LAW
These Terms are governed by the laws of [State/Jurisdiction]. Disputes shall be resolved in the courts of [State/Jurisdiction].

15. CONTACT
[Business Name], [Address], [Email], [Phone]
